scope: Pass B full-surface expansion (verified against the real CircleCI v2 OpenAPI 3.0.3 spec fetched from https://circleci.com/api/v2/openapi.json on 2026-07-04, 111 documented endpoints across every resource group). Covered: 9 read streams (projects, pipelines, workflows, jobs, contexts, schedules, checkout_keys, environment_variables, insights_workflow_summary) and 7 write actions covering schedule/environment-variable/checkout-key create-delete lifecycle. Excluded, by category: single-object detail endpoints duplicating an already-covered list stream; live-CI-state mutations (trigger/cancel/approve/rerun a pipeline, workflow, or job) that are not safe reverse-ETL targets; org/account-tier administration (organizations, groups, OIDC claims, policy/decision audit, URL Orb allow-list, usage exports, OTel exporters, deploy components/environments) out of scope for this project-scoped connector; and webhook/pipeline-definition/OIDC-claims management, which requires a project UUID this bundle's project-slug-based config has no way to resolve without an additional live lookup this dialect cannot express declaratively.

excluded.reason: permanently deletes a CircleCI project's configuration and history; not a safe reverse-ETL target
returns the current user's subset of pipelines already covered by the project's own /pipeline listing (the pipelines stream)
single-pipeline detail by pipeline number; identical record shape to the pipelines list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
triggers a new CI pipeline run; mutates live CI state, not a safe reverse-ETL target (matches legacy's own read-only scope)
the recommended successor to the legacy pipeline-trigger endpoint above; same live-CI-state-mutation exclusion applies
project-level CI configuration settings (build behavior, advanced settings); not an object list/detail resource this migration's stream model targets; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
mutates project-level CI configuration/build behavior; paired with the excluded GET settings endpoint above
cross-project pipeline listing filtered by org-slug/mine; the project-scoped /project/{project-slug}/pipeline endpoint (the pipelines stream) already covers this project's pipelines
single-pipeline detail by ID; identical record shape to the pipelines list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
returns the raw/compiled YAML config text for a pipeline, not a structured syncable object; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
returns the resolved pipeline-parameter key-value map for a single pipeline run, not an independently syncable object; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
resumes a setup-workflow pipeline paused for dynamic config continuation; a live-CI-orchestration action, not a reverse-ETL write
single-workflow detail; identical record shape to the jobs stream's parent workflow context and already reachable via the workflows stream's own list
approves a held CI workflow step; mutates live CI state, not a safe reverse-ETL target
cancels a running workflow; mutates live CI state, not a safe reverse-ETL target
reruns a workflow's jobs; mutates live CI state, not a safe reverse-ETL target
single-job detail by job ID; identical record shape to the jobs stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
cancels a running job by job ID; mutates live CI state, not a safe reverse-ETL target
cancels a running job by job number; same live-CI-state-mutation exclusion as the job-ID cancel endpoint above
single-context detail; identical record shape to the contexts list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
creates a new context; the context is org-scoped but this bundle only derives an owner-slug for reading, not creating, contexts; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
permanently deletes a context and all secrets stored under it, breaking every pipeline that references it; not a safe reverse-ETL target

lists only environment-variable NAMES (values are never returned) scoped under a single already-enumerated context; a thin child listing of the contexts stream's own record, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage

writes a secret VALUE into a shared context consumed by every pipeline using it; a secret-bearing mutation this connector's writes.json deliberately does not carry (secrets never flow through record-shaped write bodies in this dialect)
removes a secret from a shared context; paired with the excluded PUT above for the same reason
lists project-restriction rules scoped under a single context; a thin child listing, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
creates a context project-restriction rule; paired with the excluded GET above
deletes a context project-restriction rule; paired with the excluded GET/POST above
single-schedule detail by ID; identical record shape to the schedules list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-checkout-key detail by fingerprint; identical record shape to the checkout_keys list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single masked-environment-variable detail by name; identical record shape to the environment_variables list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

recent individual pipeline/workflow runs for one workflow name; a differently-shaped, higher-cardinality time-series resource than the already-covered per-workflow summary stream; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
per-job metrics summary nested under one workflow name; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
trend-annotated variant of the same per-workflow metrics already covered by insights_workflow_summary
per-test-case timing/flake metrics nested under one workflow name; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
lists branch names with recent activity for a project, not an independently useful syncable object; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
flaky-test detection report; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
paginated cross-workflow/branch summary aggregating the same metrics already covered by insights_workflow_summary
time-bucketed job-duration series nested under one workflow name; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
org-wide cross-project summary; this connector is project-scoped, not org-scoped, for its other streams
current-user identity check used only by the check request, not a syncable object stream
lists the organizations/VCS accounts the current API-token user collaborates on; account-discovery metadata, not project data
single-user detail by id; same shape as the /me identity check, not a syncable object list

requires scope-id, a project UUID this bundle's project-slug/vcs_type+org+repo config has no declarative way to resolve (CircleCI's API exposes no slug-to-UUID lookup endpoint this dialect could chain to)
single-webhook detail; same project-UUID-resolution gap as the webhook list endpoint above
same project-UUID-resolution gap as the webhook list endpoint; also carries a signing_secret in its response, a secret-bearing shape this dialect's writes.json avoids
same project-UUID-resolution and secret-bearing-response gap as webhook creation
same project-UUID-resolution gap as the other webhook endpoints
account/org-tier administration; this connector is project-scoped, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
creates a brand-new CircleCI organization/account; an account-provisioning action far outside this project-scoped connector's authority
permanently deletes an entire CircleCI organization and all its projects/history; not a safe reverse-ETL target
provisions a brand-new CircleCI project under an organization; an account-provisioning action outside this connector's per-project write scope
org-tier security-policy allow-list administration; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
modifies org-tier security policy (which URLs orbs may fetch); outside this project-scoped connector's authority
same org-tier security-policy scope as the allow-list create endpoint above
org-tier user-group administration; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
single-group detail; same org-tier scope as the groups list endpoint above
creates org-tier user-management groups; outside this project-scoped connector's authority
permanently deletes an org-tier user-management group; not a safe reverse-ETL target
initiates an async usage-export job (create-then-poll-then-download); no declarative equivalent in this dialect
polls the async usage-export job initiated above; paired exclusion

creates an org-tier telemetry-export destination, which itself carries export credentials; outside this connector's per-project write scope
same org-tier telemetry-export scope as the create endpoint above
